name: Sally Yates
summary: Former Acting Attorney General who was fired after refusing to defend Trump's travel ban, saying she was not convinced it was lawful.

keyStatements.quote: I am not convinced that the defense of the executive order is consistent with these responsibilities nor am I convinced that the executive order is lawful.
keyStatements.context: Memo directing DOJ not to defend the travel ban

keyStatements.quote: We have to be on guard against a president who doesn't respect the rule of law.
keyStatements.context: Interview about rule of law and the 2020 election

keyStatements.quote: The attack on the rule of law is unprecedented and dangerous.

timeline.event: Warned about Trump's attacks on rule of law

credibility.background: Former Acting Attorney General, former Deputy Attorney General, federal prosecutor
positions: U.S. Attorney for Northern District of Georgia (2010-2015)

oppositionReasons: Said the travel ban was likely unlawful
Warned Trump did not respect the rule of law
Said attacks on rule of law were unprecedented and dangerous
endorsement: Opposes Trump's approach to justice and rule of law

sources.title: DOJ memo from Acting Attorney General Sally Q. Yates (Jan. 30, 2017)
Why former acting AG Sally Yates says rule of law is on the ballot
